title: FIB SEM Protocol for Anaerobic Ciliates to Visualize Prokaryotic Endosymbionts
author: Johana Rotterova
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.85yhy7w

steps.title: Individually pick several hundreds of cells...
steps.instruction: Individually pick several hundreds of cells with a micropipette from 2 ml of a well-grown culture of the targeted ciliate strain. Subsequently, follow fixation protocol by Rotterová et al. (2018) - see steps briefly described below or see full protocol "Transmission electron microscopy protocol for anaerobic ciliates" listed at protocols.io ( dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.85uhy6w ). Tropidoatractidae fam. nov., a Deep Branching Lineage of Metopida (Armophorea, Ciliophora) Found in Diverse Habitats and Possessing Prokaryotic Symbionts.. Protist.

steps.title: Fix picked cells with 2
steps.instruction: Fix picked cells with 2.5% (v/v) glutaraldehyde (Polysciences) and subsequently centrifuge at 800×g for 5 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Carefully replace supernatant with 1 ml of 2
steps.instruction: Carefully replace supernatant with 1 ml of 2.5% (v/v) glutaraldehyde in 0.2M SCB (Sodium Cacodylate buffer, pH 7.2) and incubate 1 hour on ice.

steps.title: Then, rinse the pellet carefully with...
steps.instruction: Then, rinse the pellet carefully with SCB three times per 15 minutes.

steps.title: Separate the resin layer with fixed cells and wash thoroughly
steps.instruction: Separate the resin layer with fixed cells and wash thoroughly.

steps.title: Select a region containing enough vertically...
steps.instruction: Select a region containing enough vertically oriented cells within the slice of resin using dissecting microscope with transmitted illumination.

steps.title: Subsequently, process the sample following a...
steps.instruction: Subsequently, process the sample following a protocol adapted from Carpenter et al. (2013), Loyola Machado et al. (2017), and Karnkowska et al. (2019) and optimized for larger cells with archaeal endosymbionts (7, 8, 9). Correlated SEM, FIB-SEM, TEM, and NanoSIMS imaging of microbes from the hindgut of a lower termite: methods for in situ functional and ecological studies of uncultivable microbes.. Microscopy and microanalysis : the official journal of Microscopy Society of America, Microbeam Analysis Society, Microscopical Society of Canada. The Symbiotic Bacterium Fuels the Energy Metabolism of the Host Trypanosomatid Strigomonas culicis.. Protist. The Oxymonad Genome Displays Canonical Eukaryotic Complexity in the Absence of a Mitochondrion.. Molecular biology and evolution.

steps.title: Cut out square around the region...
steps.instruction: Cut out square around the region of target cells, glue to the top of blank resin block. Trim and cut out the stub, leaving the height of the block smaller than 5 mm.

steps.title: Processed the sample using scanning electron...
steps.instruction: Processed the sample using scanning electron microscope with focused ion beam milling (e.g., FEI Helios NanoLab 660 G3 UC). Orient the block using low magnification and secondary electron imaging (30 kV, 0.8 nA).

steps.title: Postfix the cells with 1% OsO4...
steps.instruction: Postfix the cells with 1% OsO4 in distilled water for 1 hour on ice.

steps.title: Rinse the cells with distilled water,...
steps.instruction: Rinse the cells with distilled water, dehydrate them in a graded ethanol series from 30% to absolute ethanol, and infiltrate them with ethanol-acetone mixture, 100% acetone, and acetone-resin mixture.

steps.title: Embed cells in absolute EPON resin...
steps.instruction: Embed cells in absolute EPON resin (Poly/Bed 812, Polysciences) and let the cells get polymerized at 70 ◦C for 48 hours.

steps.title: Cut serial ultrathin sections on an...
steps.instruction: Cut serial ultrathin sections on an Ultracut E ultramicrotome (Reichert) using a diamond knife and stain with lead citrate and uranyl acetate (2–3%).

steps.title: Apply a protective layer of carbon...
steps.instruction: Apply a protective layer of carbon onto the surface, above the region of interest.

steps.title: Remove a narrow band of resin...
steps.instruction: Remove a narrow band of resin from the front of the target region using an ion beam current of 13 - 27 nA at 30 kV.

steps.title: Mill the target region of the...
steps.instruction: Mill the target region of the block using a current of 700 pA until no artefacts are present.

steps.title: Select optimal parameters and take serial...
steps.instruction: Select optimal parameters and take serial images of the targeted cell by automatic slicing (30 kV, 0.79 nA) and imaging (2 kV, 0.2 nA) of backscattered electrons.

steps.title: Select a region (10 x 10...
steps.instruction: Select a region (10 x 10 µm) with sufficient amount of mitochondrion related organelles (MROs) and symbionts for detailed serial imaging with the highest resolution.

steps.title: To ensure imaging the correct side,...
steps.instruction: To ensure imaging the correct side, glue the block to an aluminium stub and coat it with a thin layer of platinum with high vacuum sputter coater (e.g., Leica EM ACE600).
